75904.subject: Trump Puts His Weight Behind the Clarity Act
75904.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

* **[Trump](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/trump-urges-senate-to-pass-clarity-act-for-lindsey-graham)**[ urges Senate to pass the Clarity Act in honor of Sen. Graham](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/trump-urges-senate-to-pass-clarity-act-for-lindsey-graham)

* **[Cap](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/cap-cuts-its-stabledrop-airdrop-to-usd4-2m-from-usd12m-as-backlash-mounts)**[ cuts its 'Stabledrop' airdrop to $4.2M as backlash mounts](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/cap-cuts-its-stabledrop-airdrop-to-usd4-2m-from-usd12m-as-backlash-mounts)

* **[Bonzo Lend](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/bonzo-lend-loses-9m-on-hedera-in-supra-oracle-exploit)**[ loses $9M on Hedera in a Supra oracle exploit](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/bonzo-lend-loses-9m-on-hedera-in-supra-oracle-exploit)

* **[Strategy](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/strategy-sells-467m-in-mstr-shares-bitcoin-stack-steady)**[ sells $467M in MSTR shares ](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/strategy-sells-467m-in-mstr-shares-bitcoin-stack-steady)

* **[Uniswap](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/uniswap-auctions-go-live-on-robinhood-chain)**[ Auctions go live on Robinhood Chain](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/uniswap-auctions-go-live-on-robinhood-chain)

**President Trump** [urged the Senate to pass the Clarity Act](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/trump-urges-senate-to-pass-clarity-act-for-lindsey-graham), the crypto market-structure bill, framing it as a way to beat **China** on AI and digital-asset competition. He pressed for a vote in honor of the late **Senator Lindsey Graham**, the Banking Committee chair who died days earlier, ahead of a targeted floor vote.

The Clarity Act is the bill that would split oversight of digital assets between the SEC and CFTC and define when a token is a security. Presidential pressure and a memorial framing raise the odds of a vote, though the Senate math and the exact division of regulatory turf are still where the fight lives.

**Read more below!**

75903.subject: 🟪 Let those who pay (for mistakes) decide
75903.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9f199753-ca31-4e3d-9435-5e12896ecff5/BD_NL_Banner_Heading.png?t=1781793903)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e60d7cde-4250-41f8-9443-28ed51b80560/divider_transparent.png?t=1774649182)
Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3bd8a492-ff61-41db-a7f2-c821d481fd72/BD_NL_QuoteCircle.png?t=1781794162)
Caption: 

"It is so easy to be wrong — and to persist in being wrong — when the costs of being wrong are paid by others.”
_— __Thomas Sowell_



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1bb30f12-9f32-4a2a-bd50-ba35dc11d90d/image.png?t=1783977585)
Caption: 

# Let those who pay (for mistakes) decide

Thomas Sowell lived his early years in a wooden shack, with no electricity or running water, on an unpaved road in Charlotte, NC.

He was raised by a great-aunt because his father died before he was born and his mother couldn’t afford to support him.

His aunt struggled, as well. When he was nine, they moved to Harlem in New York City, where they subsisted at the edge of homelessness.

Sowell excelled at school. The first in his family to study beyond the sixth grade, he tested into the prestigious Stuyvesant High School. At 16, however, he dropped out to support himself with a series of odd jobs, including as a Western Union messenger. He tried out for the Brooklyn Dodgers. (A strong hitter, his fielding let him down.)

Drafted into the Marines in 1951, he was assigned to a base in North Carolina as a photographer. He spent the final two years of the Korean War taking photos, collecting demerits, and boxing.

After the war, he enrolled at Howard University entirely on the strength of his test scores (he still had not graduated high school). 

Disappointed by the level of academics he found there, he transferred to Harvard, where he began with Ds and Fs on his first few tests. He graduated cum laude with a bachelor’s degree in economics, writing an admiring thesis on Karl Marx. He was 28.

He earned a master's degree at Columbia before heading to the University of Chicago for a doctorate on the recommendation of Milton Friedman.

Despite being in Friendman’s orbit, Sowell wasn’t converted away from Marxism until a summer internship at the Department of Labor, where he collected evidence on the unintended consequences of a minimum wage law in Puerto Rico. 

Sowell was always an empiricist. 

He became a professor of economics, learning that he loved teaching but not universities. Mostly, he was disillusioned by the low standards he believed students were held to at schools like Cornell, Amher

75901.body:
Forbes P.M.

Автор: Forbes


Автор: Forbes

Прочитати на LinkedIn

Good evening, Israeli surveillance company Cognyte, which touts itself as the “leading alternative” to $315 billion market cap Palantir, made most of its 2025 revenue in Israel and Europe. But it’s slowly making inroads in the U.S. by selling surveillance tech—including software that claims to find leads in big police datasets and predict where crime is likely to occur in the future. Here’s what to know. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads NurPhoto via Getty Images AI Model Wars: Anthropic Extends Fable Access Again After OpenAI’s Sol Release OpenAI claimed Sol outperformed Anthropic’s Fable in coding tasks and that its rival’s model failed to test for questions involving scientific research. Jose Luis Magana via Associated Press As Investment Fraud Losses Hit $8.6 Billion, The SEC Rethinks A Job It Was Already Supposed To Be Doing A new SEC working group devoted to the fraud problem is reassuring, though it also raises an obvious question about what the agency wasn’t already doing.
